name: Makara Sankranti
description: Sun transitions into Makara rashi, marking the beginning of Uttarayana — the auspicious six-month period.
celebration: Kite flying, bonfires, sesame sweets, donate food and clothes

name: Pongal
description: Four-day harvest festival celebrating the Sun God and agricultural abundance.
celebration: Cooking pongal rice, decorating with kolam, cattle worship

name: Vasant Panchami
description: Celebration of spring season and worship of Goddess Saraswati, the deity of knowledge.
celebration: Saraswati puja, wearing yellow, initiating children into learning

name: Maha Shivaratri
description: The Great Night of Lord Shiva — celebrating his cosmic dance of creation and destruction.
celebration: Fasting, night-long vigil, Shiva Abhishekam with milk and bilva leaves

name: Holi
description: Festival of colors celebrating the victory of Prahlada over Hiranyakashipu and the arrival of spring.
celebration: Holika Dahan bonfire, playing with colors, thandai drinks

name: Ugadi
description: Hindu New Year in Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Telangana — marking the beginning of a new cosmic cycle.
celebration: Bevu-Bella (neem and jaggery), Panchanga Shravana, new clothes

name: Gudi Padwa
description: Maharashtrian New Year celebrating the coronation of Lord Rama and the creation of the universe.
celebration: Gudi hoisting, shrikhand-puri, rangoli

name: Rama Navami
description: Birth celebration of Lord Rama, the seventh avatar of Vishnu, embodiment of dharma.
celebration: Rama Katha recitation, puja, panakam distribution, fasting

name: Hanuman Jayanti
description: Birth celebration of Lord Hanuman, the epitome of devotion, strength, and selfless service.
celebration: Hanuman Chalisa recitation, oil abhishekam, prasad distribution

name: Akshaya Tritiya
description: One of the three and a half most auspicious days in the Hindu calendar. Any deed done today is eternal.
celebration: Gold purchases, starting new ventures, charity, Lakshmi puja

name: Vaisakhi
description: Harvest festival and Sikh New Year, also marking the founding of the Khalsa Panth.
celebration: Bhangra dance, gurdwara visits, nagar kirtan processions

name: Buddha Purnima
description: Triple blessed day — birth, enlightenment, and Mahaparinirvana of Gautama Buddha.
celebration: Meditation, dharma talks, lighting lanterns, vegetarian food

name: Guru Purnima
description: Sacred day to honor spiritual preceptors (Gurus) who dispel the darkness of ignorance.
celebration: Guru puja, satsang, paduka puja, Gurudakshina

name: Nag Panchami
description: Worship of serpent deities (Nagas) who are associated with water, fertility, and protection.
celebration: Naga puja, offering milk to snake idols, fasting in some regions

name: Raksha Bandhan
description: Celebration of the sacred brother-sister bond. Sisters tie rakhi for protection and brothers give gifts.
celebration: Tying rakhis, exchanging gifts, family feasts, sweets

name: Krishna Janmashtami
description: Birth celebration of Lord Krishna, the eighth avatar of Vishnu, at midnight in Mathura.
celebration: Dahi Handi, jhankis, midnight Krishna abhishekam, fasting until midnight

name: Ganesh Chaturthi
description: Grand 10-day celebration of Lord Ganesha, the remover of obstacles and lord of beginnings.
celebration: Ganesh idol installation, puja, modak offering, visarjan procession

name: Onam
description: 10-day harvest festival of Kerala celebrating the annual homecoming of King Mahabali.
celebration: Pookalam (flower rangoli), Onasadya feast, Vallam Kali boat race

name: Navaratri
description: Nine nights dedicated to the nine forms of Goddess Durga — celebrating divine feminine power.
celebration: Durga puja, garba-dandiya, Golu display, fasting, Kanya puja

name: Dussehra
description: Victory of Lord Rama over Ravana, symbolizing the triumph of dharma over adharma.
celebration: Ravana effigy burning, Ramlila performances, Mysore Dasara procession

name: Dhanteras
description: First day of Diwali — worship of Dhanvantari (god of Ayurveda) and Lakshmi for wealth.
celebration: Gold and silver purchases, Lakshmi puja, lighting diyas

name: Diwali
description: Festival of Lights — celebrating Lord Rama's return to Ayodhya after 14 years of exile.
celebration: Lakshmi-Ganesh puja, diyas and candles, fireworks, sweets, rangoli

name: Govardhan Puja
description: Worship of Govardhan Hill, which Lord Krishna lifted to protect Vrindavan from Indra's wrath.
celebration: Annakut (mountain of food offerings), cow worship, Govardhan parikrama

name: Bhai Dooj
description: Celebration of the brother-sister bond, similar to Raksha Bandhan but with different rituals.
celebration: Tika ceremony on brother's forehead, gift exchange, family feast

name: Chhath Puja
description: Four-day Vedic festival dedicated to Surya (Sun God) and Chhathi Maiya for prosperity and longevity.
celebration: Rigorous fasting, standing in water at sunrise/sunset, offering arghya

name: Kartik Purnima
description: Full moon day of the holiest month Kartik — considered as meritorious as performing 100 yajnas.
celebration: Holy river bath, temple visits, deepdan (lamp donation), Dev Diwali in Varanasi
